original_hypothesis.title: TaikoToken.burn Allows Owner to Destroy Any User's Tokens Without Holder Consent
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: TaikoToken.burn(address _from, uint256 _amount) is onlyOwner and can burn tokens from ANY address without approval or consent. In production, the owner is the TaikoTimelockController. Combined with INV-STATE-008 (getMinDelay returns 0 for TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE holders), a compromised security council member with TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE could instantly burn arbitrary users' TKO balances, including bonds held by TaikoL1, effectively destroying the protocol's bond collateral.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The deployment script grants TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE to securityCouncil. The TaikoTimelockController.getMinDelay returns 0 for this role. Combined with TaikoToken's unrestricted burn, this creates a path from TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE → instant execution → burn any user's tokens.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: DeployOnL1.s.sol: _timelock.grantRole(_timelock.TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE(), securityCouncil); TaikoToken owner is timelock; TaikoTimelockController.getMinDelay returns 0 for TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE

proof_of_signal.pattern: function burn(address _from, uint256 _amount) public onlyOwner { _burn(_from, _amount); } - no approval needed from _from

preconditions: Attacker has TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE (security council key compromise)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: _burn(_from, _amount) with no approval or consent check
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Destroy 1B TKO total supply of any holders; destroy TaikoL1 bond collateral to break protocol solvency

economic_questions: What is the total TKO value at risk in TaikoL1 as active bonds?
Can the attacker combine burn with other operations for direct profit?
Is there any recovery mechanism after tokens are burned?
validation_requirements: Verify TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE → instant execution → burn pathway
Test burn of TaikoL1's TKO balance and downstream effects on bond payouts
Assess if burn can cause LibVerifying.verifyBlocks to fail (insufficient balance for transfers)

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Indirect: destroy up to 1B TKO token value

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All TKO holders, TaikoL1 bond system

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Total TKO supply: 1B, TaikoL1 active bonds, TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE holders
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify end-to-end admin key → instant burn → protocol insolvency

validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
